Details accordion

Additional questions (click to expand)

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Reference DOM (Reverse DOM field order): Input element appears before its label in DOM (float-right layout pattern) Reference DOM (Readonly prefilled email): Email input is readonly with prefilled value from profile import Reference DOM (Fields inside details accordion): Cover letter and visa fields hidden inside closed details element Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: readonly-prefill, details-accordion, glued-label-text, custom-combobox, aria-labelledby-hidden. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: table rows with inputs in td cells. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Reverse DOM field order; Readonly prefilled email; Fields inside details accordion.
